title: Node Exporter Code-Gen Quality Bugs
description: The node exporter produces Python code that fails at runtime for several node patterns. Discovered when exporting the Blocker group with split_frames — downstream sub-group failures prevent the main Blocker from building. Five distinct issues identified.
definition-of-done: 1. All five bug classes are fixed. 2. Blocker exports with split_frames and all generated sub-groups build without errors. 3. blender-cli validate shows no errors for the reconstructed Blocker group.
testing-plan: 1. Export Blocker with --split-frames. 2. Copy generated files to geo_node_groups/armor/blocker/. 3. Restart Blender and verify zero errors in terminal. 4. Run blender-cli validate --group Blocker.
todos: TODO : Fix helper return socket treated as node — displacement.py:75 calls .inputs on a NodeSocketVector returned by a helper (e.g. vec_math_op). Emitter should use .node.inputs[] when the output_expr is a socket alias.
TODO : Fix float-to-int default_value — piping.py sets a float on a NodeSocketInt. to_python_repr should emit int() for integer sockets.

TODO : Fix undefined variable reference — thickness.py:34 references separate_x_y_z_003 which was never assigned. Likely a separate_xyz helper whose variable was consumed but still referenced by a later link.

TODO : Fix NodeGroupInput virtual socket links — BlockerChest emits links from Group Input.outputs[0] which is a virtual/dynamic socket slot. The exporter should skip or remap virtual sockets on NodeGroupInput/NodeGroupOutput.

TODO : Fix CaptureAttribute virtual socket links — GeneralCollarShape emits links to/from CaptureAttribute dynamic input/output slots (inputs[1], outputs[1]) that only exist after an item is added. The exporter needs to handle capture_items or skip virtual sockets.
unknowns: Are there other node types with dynamic/virtual sockets beyond NodeGroupInput, NodeGroupOutput, and CaptureAttribute (e.g. RepeatZone, IndexSwitch)?
Should to_python_repr infer the target socket type from context, or should _emit_raw_node cast based on socket.type?
